title: EEG, ECG and pupil data from young and older adults: rest and auditory cued reaction time tasks
description: This dataset comprises synchronized electroencephalography (EEG), electrocardiography (ECG), and pupillography recordings from 75 participants (36 young and 39 older adults) during auditory cued reaction time tasks and passive listening conditions. Participants performed a simple reaction time task and a go/no-go task, each consisting of 120 trials with cue-only, cue-target, and cue-stimulus variations. The study investigates age-related differences in neural, cardiac, and pupillary responses during perceptual decision-making and motor preparation.
methods_description: EEG was recorded from 64 scalp electrodes using a Neuroscan system at 500 Hz sampling rate according to the International 10-20 standard. ECG was recorded bipolarly from chest electrodes. Pupil data was acquired using an iView X Hi-Speed 1250 system (SMI) at 240 Hz. Auditory stimuli were pure tones (cue: 1500 Hz, go: 1700 Hz, no-go: 1300 Hz, feedback: 1000 Hz) with 250 ms duration presented at approximately 67 dB(A). Electrode positions were digitized using a Polhemus Fastrak 3D-digitizer. All three modalities were synchronized and stored in EEGLAB .set files.
